# re/Human

> In the age of AI, your only unfair advantage is your people. re/Human builds systems that scale authentic connection.

re/Human (by Unspecified Software Co.) is a community architecture firm. We design and deploy community-driven ecosystems for brands that want to turn audiences into lasting, self-sustaining communities.

## What We Do

- Community architecture: designing systems that scale authentic human connection
- Ecosystem diagnostics: mapping current community infrastructure and identifying structural gaps
- Culture design frameworks: building the rituals, rhythms, and systems that make communities thrive
- Deployment execution: hands-on implementation of community strategies

## Founded By

Clark Sell — community builder, speaker, and systems thinker. Over two decades of experience building developer communities, conferences (THAT Conference), and human-first technology systems.

## Key Pages

- [Homepage](https://joinrehuman.com/): Overview of re/Human and what we build
- [The Manifesto](https://joinrehuman.com/thesis/): Our thesis on why community is the last satisfying moat
- [The Architect](https://joinrehuman.com/the-architect/): About Clark Sell, founder of re/Human
- [Deployments](https://joinrehuman.com/deployments/): Case studies of community architecture projects
- [Apply](https://joinrehuman.com/apply/): Apply to work with re/Human
- [Book a Session](https://joinrehuman.com/book/): Schedule a private architecture session

## Core Beliefs

- Community is the new moat — the only unfair advantage in the age of AI
- Audiences are rented; communities are owned
- Systems beat tactics — scalable connection requires architecture, not hacks
- Human connection is the product, not the byproduct
